strengths: 15+ years of continuous software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, satisfying the minimum experience threshold
Active AI infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) and open-source entrepreneurial track record with GISCollective demonstrate builder credibility
Demonstrated community leadership through public talks, workshops, and open-source tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) suggests some capacity for external influence and knowledge sharing
concerns: AI involvement is extremely recent (April 2025, ~2 months) and described at a contributor level — no evidence of having architected or shipped AI-powered products or platforms at scale

No director-level individual contributor experience; career arc is consistently Senior/Staff developer with no evidence of cross-organizational influence, strategic advisory work, or owning a technical vision at company scale

No advanced degree in an AI-related field, and the skills list lacks cloud platform depth (AWS CDK is the only cloud tool mentioned, used for a pricing feature) — both are preferred qualifications central to this role's strategy function
response.justification: While the candidate meets the minimum years-of-experience bar and shows genuine breadth as a craftsman-style engineer with entrepreneurial instincts, the role demands a director-level IC who can shape AI strategy, advise enterprise customers, and drive platform-level thinking — none of which are evidenced in this resume. AI engagement is limited to two months in a current role at the contributor level, cloud expertise is shallow, and there is no signal of the strategic influence, executive stakeholder engagement, or recognized AI thought leadership the position requires. The profile fits a strong senior/staff engineer, not a Principal Engineer operating at this scope.
